phrases.intent_en: Ask if you can swim in the lake here
phrases.text_local: Peut-on se baigner dans le lac ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: puh-TOHN suh beh-NYAY don luh LAHK ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: The Bains des Pâquis is a beloved public bathing pier on Lake Geneva, open for swimming from May to September. Entry is very affordable.

responses.english: Yes, the swimming area is open.
responses.phonetic: wee, lah ZOHN duh beh-NYAD eh oo-VEHRT
responses.local: Oui, la zone de baignade est ouverte.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the sauna and hammam
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un sauna ou un hammam ouvert toute l'année ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee-ah-TEEL un SOH-nah oo un ah-MAM oo-VAIR toot lah-NAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Bains des Pâquis has a sauna and hammam open year-round, making it a local favourite even in winter when the pier extends dramatically into the grey lake.

responses.english: It's in the basement. Ten francs.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what the breakfast offerings are
phrases.pronunciation: kuh SAIR-tohn oh puh-TEE day-zhuh-NAY ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: The Bains des Pâquis café serves a legendary weekend breakfast — a cherished Genevan ritual enjoyed overlooking the lake.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a locker for your belongings
phrases.text_local: Est-il possible de louer un casier pour mes affaires ?
phrases.pronunciation: eh-TEEL poh-SEE-bluh duh loo-AY un kah-ZYAY poor may zah-FAIR
phrases.cultural_note: Lockers and changing cabins are available on the pier for a small deposit. Towel rental is also available at the reception.

responses.english: Near the entrance, with a token.
responses.phonetic: preh duh lahn-TRAY, ah-VEK uhn zhuh-TOHN
responses.local: Près de l'entrée, avec un jeton.

phrases.intent_en: Comment on the view of the Jet d'Eau from the pier
phrases.text_local: La vue sur le Jet d'Eau depuis la jetée est magnifique !
phrases.pronunciation: lah VOO sur luh ZHEH DOH duh-PWEE lah zhuh-TAY eh mahn-yee-FEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The Bains des Pâquis pier offers one of the best views of the Jet d'Eau — a favourite spot for photographers and picnickers alike.
